title: nil pointer error when image can't be found
body:
it looks like ultimately a connection reset lead to the nil ptr error: ``` time="2018-10-16T14:47:26Z" level=debug msg="Ping https://registry.access.redhat.com/v2/ err Get https://registry.access.redhat.com/v2/: read tcp 172.16.6.40:33050->209.132.182.63:443: read: connection reset by peer (&url.Error{Op:\"Get\", URL:\"https://registry.access.redhat.com/v2/\", Err:(*net.OpError)(0xc4203e7680)})" time="2018-10-16T14:47:26Z" level=debug msg="GET https://registry.access.redhat.com/v1/_ping" panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference ``` I would expect this to instead fail gracefully with a clear "failure to pull image" error. full build log: https://openshift-gce-devel.appspot.com/build/origin-ci-test/logs/release-openshift-origin-installer-e2e-gcp-4.0/833#featurebuilds-multi-stage-image-builds-should-succeed-conformance-suiteopenshiftconformanceparallelminimal ``` time="2018-10-16T14:47:21Z" level=debug msg="reading local Dockerfile \"/tmp/build/inputs/Dockerfile\"" time="2018-10-16T14:47:21Z" level=debug msg="FROM \"centos:7 AS test\"" STEP 1: FROM centos:7 AS test time="2018-10-16T14:47:21Z" level=debug msg="error parsing image name \"localhost/centos:7\" as given, trying with transport \"docker://\": Invalid image name \"localhost/centos:7\", unknown transport \"localhost/centos\"" time="2018-10-16T14:47:21Z" level=debug msg="parsed reference into comments.body: This looks exactly the same as #1079 – are you running a post-#1082 version?
@mtrmac you can compare what we are running here: https://github.com/openshift/builder/tree/master/vendor/github.com/containers

> This looks exactly the same as #1079 – are you running a post-#1082 version?

> @mtrmac you can compare what we are running here: https://github.com/openshift/builder/tree/master/vendor/github.com/containers

Yeah, that’s is behind. It seems the repo is vendoring the https://github.com/nalind/image/commits/ip-registry fork of containers/image; update to the current version of it.
I think we're on that because @nalind is carrying a patch. @nalind can you see about getting your patch into the main repo and bumping our deps?

I was unclear: it’s not necessary to leave the branch — the current version of that branch is already up-to-date; the version vendored into openshift/builder is an older version of that branch.

Stay on that branch if you need to, only update to its latest rebase.
closing in anticipation of the dep update fixing it.
